filter_D_set_level_diversity.verdict: PASS — all 3 are non-AU; the set is over-diversified geographically and underrepresents the AU community that BAustMS serves. After the recommended replacements one AU reviewer (Shparlinski) should be added.

subfield_diversity_check.verdict: WEAK-PASS — the set is 2/3 weighted toward the PSLQ-algorithmic community (Bailey, Broadhurst) and only 1/3 toward continued-fraction analysis. Worse, both PSLQ-cluster members are in the same coauthorship network. The set after replacement should rebalance toward (a) Diophantine approximation / continued fractions in finite-precision regimes, (b) computational number theory more broadly, (c) continued-fraction metric theory.

clustering_check_no_two_same_group.shared_coauthor_group_detail: Bailey and Broadhurst are direct two-author paper coauthors (Bailey-Broadhurst 2001, Math. Comp. 70, 1719--1736, manuscript ref [3]). This is a single coauthorship cluster providing two of the three suggested reviewers -- a major clustering failure.
clustering_check_no_two_same_group.shared_recent_grant: not investigated; the Bailey-Broadhurst paper cluster is sufficient finding
clustering_check_no_two_same_group.verdict: FAIL — 2 of 3 reviewers are in the same direct-coauthorship cluster. This is doubly disqualifying given that both individuals also fail Filter B independently.

exp_math_editorial_overlap_set_level.any_member_on_em_board: UNVERIFIED — direct fetch 403; secondary searches found no evidence for any of the 3.

overall_set_evaluation: FAIL on clustering (Filter B doubly). After applying the per-reviewer recommendations (REPLACE Bailey, REPLACE Broadhurst, KEEP Vallée), the recommended replacement set is {Vallée, Bugeaud, Shparlinski} which passes all set-level checks (no shared coauthor group, no shared institution, geographic mix FR+FR+AU, subfield mix CF-metric-theory + Diophantine-approximation-CF + computational-NT).
